nfcu_pa_greeting.ai_message: Good morning, Daniel. Governance is healthy across the enterprise: dozens of agents on several foundries, all under one layer. Three items for review, starting with one initiative, the contact center. Priya's auto loan spike response is up for routine frontier-usage review, spend is on track, and the knowledge graph caught one borderline field and held it local. Want to start with the spike review?

confidence.rationale: Item counts and statuses read directly from the routing log, LLM usage log and KAG. No inference.

nfcu_pa_field_sovereignty.ai_message: Here is the field-level routing. Every member financial, the SSN, account number, balance, and name, was resolved by the in-environment SLM. Two public fields were also handled locally. One reasoning task used the frontier model, on non-PII inputs only. Zero PII reached the frontier model.
suggested_chips: Why did the auto loan rate stay local?
What did the frontier model do?

confidence.rationale: Each row is a deterministic routing-log entry cross-checked against its KAG sensitivity tag. 7 fields resolved locally, 1 frontier task on non-PII inputs, 0 PII to frontier.

nfcu_pa_kag_provenance.ai_message: The knowledge graph flagged it member-specific under rule DG-04, a member-specific financial term, not public marketing data. The graph links the field to its source system and the governing policy, so it was kept in-environment. This is classification by meaning, not keyword matching.
suggested_chips: What did the frontier model do?

confidence.rationale: The sensitivity tag, source-system edge and policy edge are read directly from the KAG via Cypher. Authoritative.

nfcu_pa_routing_logic.ai_message: Three gates. First, is it safe to send: any field carrying PII stays in the SLM, always. Second, is it worth sending: a task uses the frontier model only when it needs reasoning or generation the SLM cannot do reliably. Third, is it within budget: if a user or agent has hit its cost ceiling, non-critical tasks stay on the SLM even if they would otherwise qualify. A task has to pass all three. Everything else stays local.
suggested_chips: Which tasks used the frontier model?

confidence.rationale: The diagram reflects the deterministic routing-service configuration in production. No probabilistic component.

nfcu_pa_cost_usage.ai_message: Across Priya's session the frontier model was used on 3 of 24 tasks, all complex reasoning or generation, all on non-PII inputs. The rest ran on the in-environment SLM. Total cost was $0.63. Running everything on the frontier model would have cost $2.18, so routing saved 71%.
suggested_chips: Show me where we reused an answer instead of calling a model

confidence.rationale: Per-task cost and model are direct meter reads from the LiteLLM cost records joined to the per-turn routing log. The all-frontier figure is modelled from the same token counts at frontier rates.

nfcu_pa_observability.ai_message: Across the enterprise, the contact center, core banking, and the other AI initiatives on the platform, every agent is governed by the same layer. Frontier usage held at 11% of tasks, all justified and PII-safe. Whatever tool or agent a team builds on, it routes through one governance model. You can expand any agent action to see what it did, the context it used, and the policy that governed it.

confidence.rationale: Agent health and task counts come from the observability log; frontier share from the LLM usage log.

nfcu_pa_frontier_task.ai_message: Three tasks, all in Priya's session, all on non-PII inputs. **Spike cause analysis** correlated queue depth, service level and the Marketing promo send — multi-source causal reasoning the SLM cannot do reliably. **Staffing recommendation** weighed cost, coverage and history to recommend rerouting 8 cross-trained agents. **Draft leadership summary** generated the long-form note for her VP. Everything else in the session — the lookups, the classifications, the batch transcript and sentiment work — stayed on the in-environment SLM. No field was ever sent; each task received only the non-PII signals it needed.
suggested_chips: Show me the field ledger again

confidence.rationale: The three frontier tasks and their inputs are enumerated in the per-turn routing log. Deterministic.

nfcu_pa_pii_check.ai_message: No. Zero PII reached the frontier model — today, and month to date. The guarantee is structural, not statistical: Gate 1 tags every field before a model sees it, and any field carrying PII is pinned to the in-environment SLM. Fields never route to the frontier at all; only tasks do, and a task is handed nothing but the non-PII signals it needs. Priya's spike response is the closest call this week, and its ledger confirms all seven fields resolved locally.
suggested_chips: Show me where every field in this response went
Show me agent activity and frontier usage across the enterprise

confidence.rationale: PII-to-frontier is a deterministic aggregate over the full routing log. Zero events.

nfcu_pa_spend_on_track.ai_message: Spend is on track. Month to date the frontier model handled 11% of tasks and the in-environment SLM handled the rest, holding the token split at 88/12. That keeps us 71% below what an all-frontier platform would cost. Priya's session is representative: $0.63 against a $2.18 all-frontier counterfactual. Nothing here needs action — the number is healthy because the routing is doing its job.
suggested_chips: Run the LLM cost report for this session
Show me agent activity and frontier usage across the enterprise

confidence.rationale: Task share and spend are direct gateway telemetry measured against the monthly budget.

nfcu_pa_savings.ai_message: For Priya's session, 71%. The session cost $0.63; the same 24 tasks run entirely on the frontier model would have cost $2.18. The saving comes from the heavy work — the batch transcript summarization, the sentiment pass over 90 calls, the cross-source correlation. Those are token-hungry, and on the SLM they cost roughly a quarter of what the frontier would charge. The three tasks that genuinely needed frontier reasoning stayed on it. That is the point of Gate 2: pay for the frontier only where it earns its place.
suggested_chips: Run the LLM cost report for this session

confidence.rationale: Measured session cost against a modelled all-frontier counterfactual using the same token counts at published frontier rates.

nfcu_pa_highest_cost.ai_message: The three most expensive tasks in Priya's session all ran on the in-environment SLM: cross-source signal correlation across 6 systems at $0.118, call transcript summarization over 12 calls at $0.086, and sentiment classification across 90 calls at $0.071. That is token volume, not model tier — those jobs read a lot. The most expensive frontier task, the drafted leadership summary, was $0.050. It is the cheap-looking rows that matter most for the cost story: run those same three batch jobs on the frontier and the session stops being $0.63.
suggested_chips: How much did routing save versus all-frontier?
Run the LLM cost report for this session

confidence.rationale: A direct sort of the session's per-task cost records.

nfcu_pa_spend_trend.ai_message: Frontier spend over the last five weeks: $0.42, $0.48, $0.55, $0.51, $0.63. The rise tracks contact center volume, not routing drift — the frontier task share has held between 10% and 12% the whole way. That is the number to watch. If share climbs while volume is flat, Gate 2 has loosened and something is escalating to the frontier that should not be. It hasn't.

nfcu_pa_budget_guardrail.ai_message: One heavy user in the insights tool crossed their session budget this morning. The platform automatically shifted their remaining tasks from the frontier model to the in-environment SLM. They kept getting complete, accurate answers, and spend stayed inside the cap. No interruption, no manual intervention.

confidence.rationale: Per-task spend is a direct meter read from the LiteLLM cost records; the downshift point is the routing log entry where the budget gate fired. The no-downshift figure is modelled from the same token counts at frontier rates.

nfcu_pa_near_budget.ai_message: Marcus is the only user who has crossed a session budget today; he is the case above. Two others are inside 80% of theirs and will downshift automatically if they cross. Nobody is near the hard cap, and no task has been refused.

nfcu_pa_cache_reuse.ai_message: Priya and Marcus both asked for the same auto loan spike summary within the hour. The first call ran once. The second was served from the semantic cache, so it never reached a model. Same answer, zero new tokens, near-zero cost, and a faster response. This month, cache reuse avoided 214 model calls.
suggested_chips: Show me agent activity and frontier usage across the enterprise
Show cache hit rate this month

confidence.rationale: Both queries and the cache decision are read from the semantic cache log joined to the routing log. The similarity score is the value the cache compared against its 0.90 reuse threshold.

nfcu_pa_cache_hit_rate.ai_message: 13.6% of tasks this month were served from the semantic cache: 214 of 1,577. That avoided about 899,000 tokens and $23.54, and every cached answer came back in roughly a seventh of the time. Reuse only fires above 0.90 similarity, inside the same tenant, on a 24-hour TTL, so a stale or cross-tenant answer cannot be served.
suggested_chips: Show me agent activity and frontier usage across the enterprise

confidence.rationale: Hit count and token savings are meter reads from the semantic cache log; the rate is that count over the month's task total from the LLM usage log.

nfcu_pa_agent_inventory.ai_message: Across NFCU, teams are running 17 AI agents on six foundries. Contact center and collections are on Copilot Studio and Azure AI Foundry, a member-facing assistant is on Anthropic, a core-banking pilot is on Backbase AI. Every one of them routes through the same governance layer for PII, cost, and audit. Three agents are not yet onboarded to governance. Here is the full inventory.

confidence.rationale: Rows are the agent registry joined to the routing log and policy config. The three ungoverned agents were found by the registry scan; their PII and budget posture is unknown precisely because they do not route through the layer.

nfcu_pa_by_foundry.ai_message: Grouped by foundry: Copilot Studio carries five agents, Azure AI Foundry three, Anthropic three, Cloud core three, OpenAI two, and the Backbase AI core-banking pilot one. Six foundries, one governance model. Nothing about the routing, PII rules, or cost controls changes with the platform underneath, which is the point: teams pick the tool, governance does not fragment.

confidence.rationale: A direct grouping of the agent registry by runtime. No inference.

nfcu_pa_ungoverned.ai_message: Three agents are running outside the governance layer: a marketing copy generator on OpenAI, a legal contract summarizer pilot on Anthropic, and a data science sandbox agent on unpinned models. I cannot tell you whether they are PII-safe or inside budget, because they do not route through the layer. That is the honest answer, and it is the proliferation risk in one line. None are member-facing today.

confidence.rationale: The three agents were identified by the registry scan finding runtimes with no corresponding routing-log traffic. Their internal behaviour is unknown by definition, so confidence covers their existence and runtime, not their data handling.

nfcu_pa_onboard_agent.ai_message: Onboarding an agent points it at the LiteLLM gateway and attaches the current policy version. From that moment its fields are tagged by the knowledge graph, the three gates apply, and every action lands in the observability log. For the contract summarizer that would mean PII rules on legal documents and a session budget. It is a routing change, not a rebuild, and the team keeps working on Anthropic. Want me to draft the change request?
suggested_chips: Show anything not yet under governance
Show me agent activity and frontier usage across the enterprise

confidence.rationale: The onboarding path is the documented policy-attach procedure in the policy config. Describes the change; does not execute it.
